result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            // Should fail at first layer (file reading)\n            assert!(error.is_io());\n            assert!(error.context.contains(\"while reading file\"));\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error at file reading layer\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_accumulation_workflow() {\n    // Simulate multiple errors in a single document\n    let errors = vec![\n        ParseError::syntax(\"invalid indentation\")\n            .with_line(5)\n            .with_column(4)\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\"),\n        ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n            .with_line(10)\n            .with_column(12)\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\"),\n        ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n            .with_line(10)\n            .with_column(12)\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\"),\n    ];\n\n    assert_eq!(errors.len(), 3);\n\n    // Verify each error\n    assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n    assert!(errors[1].is_type_mismatch());\n    assert!(errors[2].is_validation());\n\n    // Verify all have the same path\n    assert_eq!(errors[0].path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(errors[1].path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(errors[2].path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Error Formatting Integration\n// ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_report_generation_workflow() {\n    let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid escape sequence\")\n        .with_path(\"config/app.yaml\")\n        .with_location(8, 25)\n        .with_context(\"while parsing application name\")\n        .with_snippet(\"app:\\n  name: \\\"My\\\\nApp\\\"\");\n\n    // Generate different report formats\n    let summary = error.summary();\n    let detailed = error.detailed_report();\n    let structured = error.format_structured();\n    let display = format!(\"{}\", error);\n    let debug = format!(\"{:?}\", error);\n\n    // Verify each format contains expected information\n    assert!(summary.contains(\"config/app.yaml:8:25\"));\n    assert!(summary.contains(\"syntax error: invalid escape sequence\"));\n    assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing application name\"));\n\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"error:\"));\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"context:\"));\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"My\\\\nApp\"));\n\n    assert!(structured.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n    assert!(structured.contains(\"config/app.yaml:8:25\"));\n    assert!(structured.contains(\"line: Some(8)\"));\n\n    assert!(display.contains(\"config/app.yaml:8:25\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n\n    assert!(debug.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n    assert!(debug.contains(\"kind\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_logging_workflow() {\n    let errors = vec![\n        ParseError::io(\"permission denied\").with_path(\"/etc/config.yaml\"),\n        ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\").with_path(\"user.yaml\").with_line(10),\n        ParseError::validation(\"invalid port\").with_path(\"service.yaml\").with_line(5),\n    ];\n\n    // Simulate logging each error\n    let log_entries: Vec<String> = errors\n        .iter()\n        .map(|e| e.summary())\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(log_entries.len(), 3);\n    assert!(log_entries[0].contains(\"I/O error\"));\n    assert!(log_entries[1].contains(\"syntax error\"));\n    assert!(log_entries[2].contains(\"validation error\"));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Result Type Integration\n// ===========================================================================\n\nfn returns_result_ok() -> Result<String> {\n    Ok(\"success\".to_string())\n}\n\nfn returns_result_err() -> Result<String> {\n    Err(ParseError::validation(\"failed validation\"))\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_integration_ok() {\n    let result = returns_result_ok();\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"success\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_integration_err() {\n    let result = returns_result_err();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    match result {\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_validation());\n            // The error message is in the kind, not context\n            assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"failed validation\"));\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_with_question_operator() {\n    fn function_with_question() -> Result<String> {\n        let value = returns_result_ok()?;\n        Ok(value)\n    }\n\n    let result = function_with_question();\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"success\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_with_question_operator_error() {\n    fn function_with_question() -> Result<String> {\n        let value = returns_result_err()?;\n        Ok(value)\n    }\n\n    let result = function_with_question();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_validation());\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n    }\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Real-World Error Scenarios\n// is invalid\".to_string());\n\n    let parse_error = match custom_error {\n        CustomError::InvalidConfig(msg) => {\n            ParseError::validation(msg)\n                .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n                .with_line(10)\n                .with_context(\"while validating custom configuration\")\n        }\n        CustomError::MissingField(field) => {\n            ParseError::validation(format!(\"missing required field: {}\", field))\n                .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n                .with_context(\"while checking required fields\")\n        }\n            })\n79\t    }\n80\t\n81\t    // Test type mismatch error creation\n82\t    let invalid_value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(\"port: \\\"8080\\\"\").unwrap();\n83\t    let test_error_creation_from_nested_parsing_context() {\n95\t    /// Simulates nested structure parsing with error accumulation\n96\t    fn extract_nested_field(value: &serde_yaml::Value, path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n97\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').collect();\n98\t        let mut current = value;\n99\t\n100\t        for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {\n101\t            match current.get(part) {\n102\t                Some(next) => current = next,\n103\t                None => {\n104\t                    return Err(ParseError::validation(\n105\t                        format!(\"missing field: {}\", part)\n106\t                    )\n107\t                    .with_context(format!(\n108\t                        \"while navigating nested structure at level {} (path: .with_location(10, 12),\n246\t    ];\n247\t\n248\t    let formatted_errors: Vec<String> = errors\n249\t        .iter()\n250\t        .map(|e| format!(\"{}\", e))\n251\t        .collect();\n252\t\n253\t    // Verify each error is properly formatted\n254\t    assert_eq!(formatted_errors.len(), 3);\n255\t\n256\t    // First error - syntax\n257\t and converting I/O errors\n    fn read_config_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n        std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::io(e.to_string())\n assert!(error.context.contains(\"/nonexistent/config.yaml\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_creation_from_yaml_parsing_context() {\n    /// Simulates YAML parsing with syntax error detection\n    fn parse_yaml_with_context(content: &str, source_path: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        serde_yaml::from_str(content)\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                let parse_err = ParseError::from(e);\n                parse_err\n                    .with_path(source_path.to_string())\n                    .with_context(\"while parsing YAML document structure\")\n            })\n    }\n\n    let invalid_yaml = \"invalid: yaml: [unclosed\";\n    let result = parse_yaml_with_context(invalid_yaml, \"config/services.yaml\");\n\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = field_path: &str) -> Result<u16> {\n        value.as_i64()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\n                field_path,\n                \"integer\",\n                value.as_str().unwrap_or(\"null\")\n            ).with_context(\"while validating port configuration\"))\n            .and_then(|port| {\n                if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n                    Err(ParseError::validation(\n                        format!(\"port {} is out of valid range (1-65535)\", port)\n                    ).with_context(\"while validating port configuration\"))\n                } else {\n                    Ok(port as u16)\n                }\n            })\n    }\n\n    // Test type mismatch error creation\n    let invalid_value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(\"port: \\\"8080\\\"\").unwrap();\n    let port_obj = &invalid_value.as_mapping().unwrap().get(\"port\").unwrap();\n    let result = validate_port_field(port_obj, \"server.port\");\n\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n\n    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch());\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while validating port\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_creation_from_nested_parsing_context() {\n    /// Simulates nested structure parsing with error accumulation\n    fn extract_nested_field(value: &serde_yaml::Value, path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').collect();\n        let mut current = value;\n\n        for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {\n            match current.get(part) {\n                Some(next) => current = next,\n                None => {\n                    return Err(ParseError::validation(\n                        format!(\"missing field: {}\", part)\n                    )\n                    .with_context(format!(\n                        \"while navigating nested structure at level {} (path: {})\",\n                        i,\n                        path\n                    )))\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        current.as_str()\n            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\n                path,\n                \"string\",\n                \"null or other type\"\n            ).with_context(format!(\"while extracting final value from path: {}\", path)))\n    }\n\n    let yaml = r#\"database:\n  connection:\n    timeout: 30\"#;\n    let value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(yaml).unwrap();\n\n    // Test successful extraction\n    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.timeout\");\n    assert!(result.is_err()); // timeout is a number, not string\n\n    // Test missing field error\n    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.missing\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while navigating nested structure\"));\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"level\"));\n}\n\n// .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n            .with_location(5, 4),\n        ParseError::type_mismatch(\"server.port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n ===========================================================================\n// Error Propagation Through Result Types\n// ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_through_parsing_pipeline() {\n    fn read_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n        std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while reading file\"))\n    }\n\n    fn parse_yaml(content: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        serde_yaml::from_str(content)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while parsing YAML\"))\n    }\n\n    fn validate_config(value: serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Simulate validation\n        if value.get(\"required_field\").is_none() {\n            return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: required_field\")\n                .with_context(\"while validating configuration structure\"));\n        }\n        Ok(value)\n    }\n\n    fn full_pipeline(path: &Path) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        let content = read_file(path)?;\n        let yaml = parse_yaml(&content)?;\n        let validated = validate_config(yaml)?;\n        Ok(validated)\n    }\n\n    // Test error propagation at file reading stage\n    let result = full_pipeline(Path::new(\"/nonexistent/config.yaml\"));\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while reading file\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_context_accumulation() {\n    fn level_3_operation() -> Result<String> {\n        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n            .with_line(10)\n            .with_column(5)\n            .with_context(\"while parsing token\"))\n    }\n\n    fn level_2_operation() -> Result<String> {\n        level_3_operation()\n            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while processing statement\"))\n    }\n\n    fn level_1_operation() -> Result<String> {\n        level_2_operation()\n            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while parsing function body\"))\n    }\n\n    let result = level_1_operation();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    // Context should be accumulated through the chain\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while parsing function body\"));\n    assert!(error.line == Some(10)); // Original line preserved\n    assert!(error.column == Some(5)); // Original column preserved\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_successful_intermediate_steps() {\n    fn step1_read() -> Result<String> {\n        Ok(\"valid: content\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    fn step2_parse(content: String) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        serde_yaml::from_str(&content)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while parsing YAML\"))\n    }\n\n    fn step3_validate(value: serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<String> {\n        value.get(\"valid\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"valid\", \"string\", \"null\")\n                .with_context(\"while extracting 'valid' field\"))\n    }\n\n    let result = step1_read()\n        .and_then(|content| step2_parse(content))\n        .and_then(|value| step3_validate(value));\n\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"content\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_question_operator() {\n    fn multi_step_pipeline(input: &str) -> Result<String> {\n        let parsed = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(input)?;\n        let field = parsed.get(\"field\")\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'field'\"))?;\n        let value = field.as_str()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"null\"))?;\n        Ok(value.to_string())\n    }\n\n    // Test with invalid YAML\n    let result = multi_step_pipeline(\"invalid: yaml: [\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    // Error should be from serde_yaml parsing\n    assert!(error.is_syntax() || error.to_string().contains(\"error\"));\n\n    // Test with valid YAML but missing field\n    let result = multi_step_pipeline(\"other: value\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.is_validation());\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"missing 'field'\"));\n\n    // Test with wrong type\n    let result = multi_step_pipeline(\"field: 123\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch());\n}\n\n// Ok(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n    }\n\n    let invalid_utf8 = b\"\\xFF\\xFE invalid\";\n    let result = parse_yaml_bytes(invalid_utf8);\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = complex_parsing_operation(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<String> {\n        // Step 1: UTF-8 conversion (Utf8Error → ParseError)\n        let content = std::str::from_utf8(bytes)?;\n\n        // Step 2: YAML parsing (serde_yaml::Error → ParseError)\n        let value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Field extraction (custom ParseError)\n        let field = value.get(\"target\")\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'target' field\"))?;\n\n        let string_value = field.as_str()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"target\", \"string\", \"other\"))?;\n\n        Ok(string_value.to_string())\n    }\n\n    // Test UTF-8 error conversion\n    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"\\xFF target: test\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"invalid UTF-8\"));\n\n    // Test YAML parsing error conversion\n    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"invalid: yaml: [\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"error\") || error.to_string().contains(\"syntax\"));\n\n    // Test custom error creation\n    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"other: value\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.is_validation());\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"missing 'target'\"));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Error Context Preservation\n// ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_context_preservation_through_multiple_layers() {\n    fn inner_parser() -> Result<String> {\n        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected token\")\n            .with_line(5)\n            .with_column(10)\n            .with_snippet(\"value: unexpected_token\"))\n    }\n\n    fn middle_processor() -> Result<String> {\n        inner_parser()\n            .map_err(|e| e\n                .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n                .with_context(\"while processing configuration file\"))\n    }\n\n    fn outer_validator() -> Result<String> {\n        middle_processor()\n            .map_err(|e| e\n                .with_context(\"while validating application configuration\")\n                .with_context(\"during startup validation\"))\n    }\n\n    let result = outer_validator();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n\n    // Original context should be preserved\n    assert_eq!(error.line, Some(5));\n    assert_eq!(error.column, Some(10));\n    assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(error.snippet, Some(\"value: unexpected_token\".to_string()));\n\n    // Context should be accumulated (note: current implementation overwrites)\n    // The final context should be from the outermost layer\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"during startup validation\") ||\n            error.context.contains(\"while validating\") ||\n            error.context.contains(\"while processing\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_context_preservation_with_snippets() {\n    let original_error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid escape sequence\")\n        .with_line(8)\n ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_real_world_config_loading_with_errors() {\n    fn load_application_config(path: &Path) -> Result<(String, u16)> {\n        // Simulate reading and parsing application configuration\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e)\n                .with_context(\"while reading application configuration file\"))?;\n\n        let yaml = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(&content)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e)\n                .with_context(\"while parsing application configuration\"))?;\n\n        let app_name = yaml.get(\"app\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| .and_then(|v| v.as_i64())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"null\")\n                .with_context(\"while extracting port number\"))?;\n\n        if port test_real_world_multi_file_config_with_error_aggregation() {\n    fn load_service_configs(paths: &[&str]) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let mut configs = Vec::new();\n\n        for path in paths {\n            let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n                .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e)\n                    .with_path((*path).to_string())\n                    .with_context(format!(\"while reading service config: {}\", path)))?;\n\n            let yaml = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(&content)\n                .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e)\n                    .with_path((*path).to_string())\n                    .with_context(format!(\"while parsing service config: {}\", path)))?;\n\n            let service_name = yaml.get(\"name\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'name' field\")\n                    .with_path((*path).to_string())\n                    .with_context(format!(\"while extracting service name from: {}\", path)))?;\n\n            configs.push(service_name.to_string());\n        }\n\n        Ok(configs)\n    }\n\n    // Test error context \"integer\", \"null\")\n41\t            .with_context(\"while extracting database port\"))?;\n42\t\n43\t    Ok(DatabaseConfig {\n44\t        host: host.to_string(),\n45\t        port: port as test_result_parse_error_compiles() {\n71\t    // Test that Result<T, ParseError> compiles and works correctly\n72\t    fn returns_parse_error() -> Result<String> {\n73\t        Ok(\"success\".to_string())\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    let result: Result<String> = returns_parse_error();\n77\t    assert!(result.is_ok());\n78\t    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), let serde_err = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(invalid_yaml).unwrap_err();\n96\t    let parse_err: ParseError = serde_err.into();\n97\t\n98\t    // Should classify as an error (syntax or Integration tests for ParseError error propagation\n//!\n//! This test suite demonstrates error propagation through a call stack using\n//! the Result<T, ParseError> pattern and the ? operator.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::Path;\n\n/// Simulates reading a configuration file\nfn read_config_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n    // This will fail since the file doesn't exist\n    // The io::Error automatically converts to ParseError via the From impl\n    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n    Ok(content)\n}\n\n/// Simulates parsing YAML content\nfn parse_yaml_content(content: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n    // This will fail if the YAML is invalid\n    // serde_yaml::Error automatically converts to ParseError via the From impl\n    let value = serde_yaml::from_str(content)?;\n    Ok(value)\n}\n\n/// Simulates validating a specific field from the parsed YAML\nfn extract_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n    // Extract database configuration with context\n    let db_section = value.get(\"database\")\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'database' section\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database configuration\"))?;\n\n    let host = db_section.get(\"host\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.host\", \"string\", \"null\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database host\"))?;\n\n    let port = db_section.get(\"port\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_i64())\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.port\", \"integer\", \"null\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database port\"))?;\n\n    Ok(DatabaseConfig {\n        host: host.to_string(),\n        port: port as u16,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Simulates a complete configuration loading workflow\nfn load_config_from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n    // This demonstrates error propagation through a call stack:\n    // 1. read_config_file can fail with io::Error → ParseError\n    // 2. parse_yaml_content can fail with serde_yaml::Error → ParseError\n    // 3. extract_database_config can fail with custom ParseError\n\n    let content = read_config_file(path)?;\n    let yaml = parse_yaml_content(&content)?;\n    let config = extract_database_config(&yaml)?;\n\n    Ok(config)\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct DatabaseConfig {\n    host: String,\n    port: u16,\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_parse_error_compiles() {\n    // Test that Result<T, ParseError> compiles and works correctly\n    fn returns_parse_error() -> Result<String> {\n        Ok(\"success\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    let result: Result<String> = returns_parse_error();\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"success\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_from_io_error() {\n    // Test that std::io::Error converts to ParseError\n    let io_err = io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, \"file not found\");\n    let parse_err: ParseError = io_err.into();\n\n    assert!(parse_err.is_io());\n    assert!(parse_err.to_string().contains(\"file not found\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_from_serde_yaml_error() {\n    // Test that serde_yaml::Error converts to ParseError\n    let invalid_yaml = \"not: valid: yaml: [\";\n    let serde_err = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(invalid_yaml).unwrap_err();\n    let parse_err: ParseError = serde_err.into();\n\n    // Should classify as an error (syntax or other depending on the error message)\n    // Just verify it converted successfully and contains error info\n    let err_str = parse_err.to_string();\n    assert!(!err_str.is_empty(), \"Error message should not be empty\");\n\n    // Verify it's one of the expected error kinds\n    assert!(parse_err.is_syntax() || parse_err.to_string().contains(\"error\"),\n            \"Error should be syntax or contain 'error': {}\", err_str);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_from_utf8_error() {\n    // Test that std::str::Utf8Error converts to ParseError\n    let invalid_utf8 = b\"\\xFF\\xFE\";\n    let utf8_err = std::str::from_utf8(invalid_utf8).unwrap_err();\n    let parse_err: ParseError = utf8_err.into();\n\n    assert!(parse_err.to_string().contains(\"invalid UTF-8\"));\n    assert!(!parse_err.context.is_empty());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_from_from_utf8_error() {\n    // Test that std::string::FromUtf8Error converts to ParseError\n    let invalid_bytes = vec![0xFF, 0xFE, 0xFD];\n    let from_utf8_err = String::from_utf8(invalid_bytes).unwrap_err();\n    let parse_err: ParseError = from_utf8_err.into();\n\n    assert!(parse_err.to_string().contains(\"invalid UTF-8\"));\n    assert!(!parse_err.context.is_empty());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_question_mark() {\n    // Test error propagation through call stack with ? operator\n    let nonexistent_path = Path::new(\"/nonexistent/config.yaml\");\n    let result = load_config_from_file(nonexistent_path);\n\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let err = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(err.is_io()); // io::Error → ParseError\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_context() {\n    // Test error propagation with context accumulation\n    let invalid_yaml = \"database:\\n  host: null\\n  port: wrong\";\n\n    let result: Result<DatabaseConfig> = (|| {\n        let content = invalid_yaml.to_string();\n        let yaml = parse_yaml_content(&content)?;\n        extract_database_config(&yaml)\n    })();\n\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let err = result.unwrap_err();\n\n    // Error should have context\n    assert!(!err.context.is_empty());\n    assert!(err.to_string().contains(\"while extracting\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_successful_propagation_chain() {\n    // Test successful propagation through the entire call stack\n    let valid_yaml = r#\"\ndatabase:\n  host: localhost\n  port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n    let result: Result<DatabaseConfig> = (|| {\n        let yaml = parse_yaml_content(valid_yaml)?;\n        extract_database_config(&yaml)\n    })();\n\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    let config = result.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(config.host, \"localhost\");\n    assert_eq!(config.port, 5432);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_builder_pattern_with_context() {\n    // Test that builder pattern works with context\n    let err = ParseError::validation(\"invalid value\")\n        .with_context(\"while parsing field 'timeout'\")\n        .with_line(42)\n        .with_column(10)\n        .with_path(\"config.yaml\");\n\n    assert_eq!(err.line, Some(42));\n    assert_eq!(err.column, Some(10));\n    assert_eq!(err.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert!(!err.context.is_empty());\n    assert_eq!(err.context, \"while parsing field 'timeout'\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_type_checking() {\n    // Test error type checking methods\n    let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n    assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n    assert!(!syntax_err.is_io());\n\n    let io_err = ParseError::io(\"test\");\n    assert!(io_err.is_io());\n    assert!(!io_err.is_syntax());\n\n    let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"test\");\n    assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n    assert!(!validation_err.is_syntax());\n\n    let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"int\");\n    assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n    assert!(!type_err.is_syntax());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_nested_error_propagation() {\n    // Test deeply nested error propagation\n    fn level_3() ->  }\n\n    let result = level_1();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let err = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(err.is_io());\n    assert!(err.to_string().contains(\"level 3
